headline: US-Iran ceasefire 'holds' despite attacks in Strait of Hormuz
summary: The US defence secretary says Iran is attempting 'international extortion' in the strait. Iran warned the US 'we are just getting started'.

headline: Hantavirus may have spread between passengers on cruise ship, WHO says
summary: Two cases of the virus, which rarely spreads between humans, have been confirmed on the ship, and three people have died.

headline: Explosion at China fireworks factory kills 26 people
summary: A blast at a fireworks factory in Hunan province has killed 26 people and injured 61, state media report.

headline: Russia declares truce in Ukraine for Victory Day; Kyiv says it'll cease fire two days earlier
summary: Russia demanded a ceasefire on 8-9 May but Ukraine said it would pause fighting sooner, meaning any violations would be pinned on Moscow.

headline: Nine coal miners die in gas explosion in Colombia
summary: The explosion is the latest accident in the locality of Sutatausa, the site of another deadly mine disaster in 2023.

headline: Two killed and many injured after car driven into crowd in German city of Leipzig
summary: A 33-year-old German citizen was detained following the incident, the authorities say.

headline: IMF cuts global growth outlook to 3.1% due to Middle East war
summary: The International Monetary Fund cut its growth outlook citing the Middle East war but said the world was already drifting toward a more adverse scenario.

headline: World Cup broadcast rights deadlock may leave millions in India and China without coverage
summary: Millions of soccer fans in the world's two most populous nations may not be able to watch the World Cup that starts next month.

headline: Film star Vijay reshapes politics in Indian state of Tamil Nadu
summary: A 'fun' superstar has defied naysayers to almost sweep Tamil Nadu polls, marking a break in the political order.

headline: The most talked-about looks at this year's Met Gala
summary: The theme for 2026 Met Gala fundraiser is 'Costume Art', exploring fashion as an embodied art form.
